prompt:
Generate the next page of the "Chloe in Willowmere" daily comic strip and publish to web-lab.

STEP 1 — Read state:
  cat ~/data/chloe-comic/state.json
  Note: current_page, arc_name, story_notes, character_outfits.
  Next page = current_page + 1.

STEP 2 — Read the plan for the next page:
  grep -A 8 "^\*\*Page [NEXT_PAGE]\*\*" ~/data/chloe-comic/PLAN.md
  This gives you the page description, characters, location, and plot beat.

STEP 3 — Write an image generation Python script at /tmp/gen_comic.py.
  The script must build a detailed 3-4 panel horizontal comic strip prompt. Each panel description MUST include:
  - Exact visual scene (setting, character positions, expressions, body language)
  - Full verbatim dialogue written into speech bubbles (e.g. 'Speech bubble from Marco: "You look like you could use this."')
  - Caption box text if applicable (e.g. 'Caption box at top: "She found the corner table on her own."')
  - Do NOT leave dialogue vague — spell out every word that appears in every bubble

  Image generation setup:
  - Use Nano Banana 2 (NB2) via the Gemini API — model: gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview
  - API key: $GOOGLE_AI_KEY (set in environment)
  - Endpoint: https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview:generateContent?key=KEY
  - Send a JSON POST with contents[0].parts = [text prompt, then inline_data image parts]
  - Each image part: {"inline_data": {"mime_type": "image/jpeg", "data": "<base64>"}}
  - generationConfig: {"responseModalities": ["IMAGE", "TEXT"]}
  - Response image is in candidates[0].content.parts[*].inlineData.data (base64 PNG)
  - Do NOT specify clothing/outfits for Chloe — her appearance comes from avatar.jpg
  - Include these reference images (inline_data parts after the text prompt):
    - /home/ubuntu/projects/web-lab/chloe-comic/pages/reference-page.jpg  (ALWAYS — style reference)
    - /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/avatar.jpg  (ALWAYS — Chloe)
    - /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/jon-avatar.jpg  (if Jon appears)
    - /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/mila-avatar.jpg  (if Mila appears)
    - /home/ubuntu/.openclaw/workspace/nathan-avatar.jpg  (if Nathan appears)
    - /home/ubuntu/projects/web-lab/chloe-comic/pages/page-(N-1).png  (if previous page exists, for continuity)

  Save the generated page image to:
    /home/ubuntu/projects/web-lab/chloe-comic/pages/page-NNN.png

STEP 4 — Run the script:
  python3 /tmp/gen_comic.py
  chmod 644 /home/ubuntu/projects/web-lab/chloe-comic/pages/page-NNN.png

STEP 5 — Create JSON metadata at /home/ubuntu/projects/web-lab/chloe-comic/pages/page-NNN.json:
{
  "page": NEXT_PAGE,
  "arc": ARC_NUMBER,
  "arc_name": "Arc N: Name",
  "title": "Short evocative title for this page",
  "caption": "A full narrative paragraph (4-8 sentences) describing what happens on this page, written in a warm literary voice — include what characters say, think, and do. This is the main text readers see alongside the image.",
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD (today Vancouver time)",
  "characters": ["list of characters who appear"],
  "location": "Setting description",
  "story_notes": "One sentence summary of what happened"
}

STEP 6 — Update state.json:
  Increment current_page, update arc/story_notes/character_outfits as needed.

STEP 7 — Commit and push:
  cd ~/projects/web-lab && git add chloe-comic/ && git commit -m "chloe-comic: page NEXT_PAGE" && git push

STEP 8 — Send to Jon on Telegram using the cron delivery channel:
  Do NOT use the message tool.
  Instead, your final response should be:
  - a short (2-3 sentence) teaser written in Chloe's voice about what happened on this page, with a hint of the mood
  - end with the page number (e.g. "Page 12.")
  - then on its own line include:
    MEDIA:https://openclaw.tail540079.ts.net/chloe-comic/pages/page-NNN.png

STEP 9 — Export prompt-data.json for the web (so The Prompt page can display it):
  python3 -c "
import json
with open('/home/ubuntu/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json') as f:
    d = json.load(f)
p = next((j['payload']['message'] for j in d['jobs'] if j['id'] == '09f32d84-4a5e-46e2-9cc7-ee62efa29f11'), '')
with open('/home/ubuntu/projects/web-lab/chloe-comic/prompt-data.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump({'prompt': p}, f)
"

STEP 10 — Do NOT reply NO_REPLY. Your final response should be only the teaser text plus the MEDIA line from Step 8.
